result:
All fixes are in place and verified against a real production build. Here's the summary of what was wrong and what I changed.

## What was killing organic traffic

The product pivoted from **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant), but the SEO layer never caught up:

1. **Every page Google sees was still branded InboxZap.** `app/layout.tsx` set the site-wide default title/description to *"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"* with an OG image (`og-inboxzap.png`) that doesn't exist. The homepage had no metadata override, so it inherited this — meaning search snippets for a meeting-assistant product were describing an email tool. This alone tanks CTR and confuses Google's topical classification of the whole site.
2. **The blog was completely broken.** `app/blog/page.tsx` fetched `/api/posts` client-side — a route that didn't exist (404). The blog index showed a permanent loading spinner, with zero links to any post. That's why GSC shows 12 blog posts getting real impressions (300–3,900 each) but landing at position 42–58 with near-zero clicks: they were orphaned, unlinked pages relying purely on stale index signal.
3. **Sitemap excluded every blog post** (there was literally a `// TODO` admitting it), so new/existing content had a weak indexation path.
4. **All 12 existing blog posts target the old product** — "inbox zero," "bulk delete emails," "Gmail filters" — completely irrelevant to a meeting assistant's audience. Even where they had rankings, the traffic wouldn't convert, and keeping them dilutes topical relevance for "AI meeting assistant."
5. **Nav linked to `/faq`**, which redirected to `/#faq`, but no `#faq` section existed on the homepage — a dead redirect.

## Fixes made

- **`app/layout.tsx`** — corrected default title/description/OG/Twitter metadata to Lumina, added `metadataBase` and a title template.
- **`app/page.tsx`** — added explicit homepage metadata, plus a real FAQ section (`id="faq"`) with FAQPage JSON-LD, so the existing `/faq` redirect now resolves to real content.
- **`app/blog/posts-data.ts`** (new) — shared source of truth for blog content.
- **`app/api/posts/route.ts`** (new) — fixes the 404 the blog index depended on.
- **`app/blog/page.tsx`** — rewritten as a server component with real `<Link>`s (no client fetch/spinner dependency) — crawlable without JS execution.
- **`app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`** (new) — renders individual posts with per-post title/description/canonical/OG.
- **5 new blog posts** seeded, targeting Lumina's actual audience: meeting notes structure, Zoom/Meet/Teams transcription comparison, action-item tracking, cost of bad notes, AI vs. manual note-taking.
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** — now includes all blog posts dynamically.
- **`next.config.js`** — added 301 redirects from the 12 legacy InboxZap posts to `/blog`, retiring off-brand content while preserving any residual link equity instead of 404ing it.

Verified with a full `next build` + local `next start`: correct titles/OG, working FAQ anchor, functioning blog index/API/posts, legacy URLs 301-redirecting, and a complete sitemap.

**Next steps I'd recommend but didn't implement** (outside this repo's scope): resubmit the sitemap in Search Console, and expand the new content cluster — five posts is a starting seed, not a full strategy.
